status: RESOLVED (previously blocked at MA SOC / OpenCorporates -- pivoted to SEC EDGAR since owner is a public REIT)

parent.name: Phillips Edison Grocery Center REIT I, Inc.
parent.note: later renamed/became Phillips Edison & Company, Inc., NASDAQ: PECO

parent_entity_chain.source: SEC Exhibit 21.1 (Subsidiaries of the Registrant), filed with Phillips Edison Grocery Center REIT I FY2014 Form 10-K
parent_entity_chain.note: Entity naming pattern "[Property Name] Station LLC" appears ~150+ times in this same exhibit -- confirmed as PECO's standard one-subsidiary-per-property holding structure, not a one-off.

sources.source: Springfield Assessor record card, Sales/Ownership History
sources.gives: exact price $31,007,400, deed type SALE OF MULTIPLE PARCELS

sources.source: Urstadt Biddle Properties Inc. SEC Form 8-K + press release (seller side)
sources.gives: identical price $31,007,400, confirms all-cash, confirms institutional buyer

sale_transaction_triple_confirmed.verdict: Three independent sources (buyer-side registry, government assessor, seller-side federal SEC filing) agree exactly on price and date. Verified tier -- as strong as this data gets.

mortgage_question_resolved.finding: No mortgage recorded against the parcel (confirmed via Hampden registry, cross-validated against a working scraper).
mortgage_question_resolved.explanation_found: Seller (Urstadt Biddle) SEC press release states the sale was "an all cash price" and that proceeds were used to repay Urstadt Biddle's own UNSECURED revolving credit facility -- consistent with no property-specific mortgage being recorded. Likely explanation: Phillips Edison financed the acquisition at the corporate/OP level (common for grocery-anchored shopping center REITs) rather than via a property-secured loan, which is why Hampden County shows no MTG document.
mortgage_question_resolved.confidence: estimated/inferred (fact_type=inferred) -- we did not find a PECO-side filing that explicitly states their financing method for this specific acquisition, only that it is consistent with the absence of a recorded mortgage.

bonus_historical_context.ownership_since_1970s: Urstadt Biddle owned the property since the early 1970s (matches our own found 1970-02-18 prior sale to Hubbard Real Estate Investments -- likely a predecessor/merged entity name, not a contradiction).
bonus_historical_context.source: Urstadt Biddle Properties Inc. SEC press release, Sept 25 2014
